lines.text: Pursuing a Bachelor of Engineering in Computer Science at RV College of Engineering (RVCE), Bengaluru (2024–2028), with a focus on probability and statistics courses.
Designed Meridian, an orchestration system using Python, FastAPI, and asyncio to produce cited due-diligence memos on NSE/BSE companies.

violations: Line 1 (from sk-edu-rvce) claims 'Pursuing a Bachelor of Engineering in Computer Science at RV College of Engineering (RVCE), Bengaluru (2024–2028), with a focus on probability and statistics courses.' — no mention of focus on probability and statistics in this line's Source.

Line 2 (from sk-proj-meridian-sys) claims 'Designed Meridian, an orchestration system using Python, FastAPI, and asyncio to produce cited due-diligence memos on NSE/BSE companies.' — no mention of using FastAPI or asyncio in this line's Source.

paragraphs: I am excited to apply for the Quantitative Trading Intern position at Castellan Capital. Your recent expansion of the systematic trading research team aligns perfectly with my background in probability and statistics, as well as my experience with Python through projects like Meridian and Anchor.

In Meridian, I designed and implemented a 4-agent orchestration system that produced cited due-diligence memos on NSE/BSE companies with minimal hallucination rates. This project showcased my proficiency in Python and deep understanding of multi-agent systems and their evaluation. Similarly, at KuKClean, I leveraged Figma to create high-fidelity mockups for product pages, honing my skills in frontend development that could be applied to visualizing backtesting results.

My goal is to join a team committed to research-driven analysis and robust backtesting, which aligns with Castellan Capital's culture of high-performance and competitive excellence. I am eager to contribute to your systematic trading strategies using my technical expertise.
cover_letter.company_detail_referenced: recent expansion of the systematic trading research team

linkedin.message: Hi — I'm applying for the Quantitative Trading Intern role and am excited about Castellan Capital’s high-performance, research-driven culture. My background in probability and Python through Meridian aligns well with your systematic trading needs.

gaps.requirement: Strong background in probability, statistics, and linear algebra

gaps.requirement: Programming proficiency in Python or C++ for quantitative research
gaps.how_to_close: Highlight experience with Python programming through projects like Meridian and Anchor, and mention a willingness to learn C++ quickly.

gaps.requirement: Currently pursuing a degree in Math, Statistics, CS, Physics, or related quantitative field
gaps.how_to_close: Stress the Computer Science background and the pursuit of a related degree at RVCE.

gaps.requirement: Experience with time-series analysis or backtesting frameworks
gaps.how_to_close: Mention interest in gaining this experience through additional projects or self-study before the application deadline.
